text: Here's what science actually says about the sodium potassium pump. This tiny protein moves sodium and potassium ions across the cell membrane. But why is this so crucial for our cells? It helps maintain the electrochemical gradient, essential for nerve impulses and muscle contractions. Without it, our bodies would struggle to function. And here’s the kicker: recent studies suggest we barely understand all its potential! Want to dive deeper into cellular science? Hit that like button and follow for more!
